eval_set.note: held-out provenance set; single-judge content+format scoring via tools/eval_local_model.py

fp16_base.note: FP16 base is deterministic; measured 23/32 content in run #5 on the identical model+eval

combined_channel.note: religion format is consistently ~1/6 across all runs (eval-format quirk, not content), capping religion's combined

moe_adapt_lora_rank_alloc.note: VERIFIED from the run's train.log [lora-rank-alloc] line. Redistribution is MODEST (15-17 around uniform 16): cheap attention modules (small fan_out) got +1, the large up_proj got -1. So the allocated and uniform-3ep adapters are near-identical in capacity at r=16 — which is WHY the +6.2pt content gain over uniform-3ep must be read as eval noise, not the allocation.

z3_solver_attestation.note: VERIFIED from the run's step-6 promotion report. The pod cloned this branch (requirements-lora.txt installs z3-solver), so all five invariants ran on backend=z3 and the W2 oracle PROMOTED — flipping run #7's reject-on-missing-z3 to a real solver-checked promote, exactly as proven offline.

result_summary: The moe/adapt-allocated 3-epoch QLoRA adapter scored 90.6% CONTENT (29/32) on the held-out provenance set — the highest of the three real-GPU runs (base 71.9%, uniform-3ep 84.4%, alloc-3ep 90.6%). It exercises BOTH new mechanisms end-to-end on hardware. The gain over the uniform 3-epoch adapter (+6.2pt) is WITHIN single-judge / n=32 / 1-seed noise and must NOT be read as the allocator outperforming uniform — it is a successful end-to-end exercise plus a directional datapoint, not a validated comparison.
claim: real-GPU LoRA eval-ladder datapoint exercising moe/adapt allocation + z3 oracle (NOT a capability claim)
judge: single-judge content+format scoring (tools/eval_local_model.py); NOT the kappa>=0.40 / 2-judge-family / >=3-run / 95%-CI VALIDATED bar
honest_scope: One real-GPU QLoRA run (3 epochs, seed 0, --lora-rank-alloc, 32-item held-out provenance eval, single judge). Confirms the moe/adapt LoRA allocator and the z3-enabled W2 oracle run end-to-end on GPU. The 90.6% content and the +6.2pt over uniform-3ep are DIRECTIONAL / candidate-only: at n=32 / single judge / one seed they are not separable from noise, and the allocator's sensitivity prior is a transparent heuristic, not a measurement. Does NOT move canClaimAGI. A validated allocator-vs-uniform comparison needs the P6 preregistration protocol (>=3 seeds, 2 non-qwen judge families, kappa>=0.40, 95% CI).

provenance.source: transcribed from the train-runpod step-7 eval-ladder report in the run log; rank_pattern + promotion verdict in the run artifact
